title: Floyd's build-heap is O(n) (TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.2.3)
statement_informal: Sift-down at a node of height j costs O(j); summed over a perfect binary tree of height h, the total work is the sum of all node heights, which is LINEAR in the node count: sheight h + h + 1 = nodes h, so sheight h <= nodes h ~ n. Build-heap is O(n), not O(n log n).

cost_model.notes: sheight h = total sift-down work over a perfect tree of height h, sheight (S h') = S h' + 2*sheight h'. nodes h = 2^(h+1)-1. The closed identity sheight h + h + 1 = nodes h (proved by structural induction) shows total work <= n.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.2.3 — Floyd's bottom-up heap construction runs in linear time; the sum of node heights in a complete tree is O(n).
The geometric series sum_{j>=1} j/2^j = 2 underlies the bound.
